_comment: Asked once per page. The judge sees two 5-panel motion strips side by side (reference + agent), each showing the page's load-triggered entrance animation at t=0/25/50/75/100 % of duration_ms. Per-panel SSIM is the Track A measurement; these questions sit on top of the same image pair.

questions.text: Does the agent's motion strip show an entrance animation on the same element as the reference (matched by position within the band)? A 5 means an unambiguous match; a 1 means no animated element is visible in the agent's strip at all.

Is the direction of motion the same in the agent's strip as in the reference (slide-up rising from below, slide-down dropping from above, slide-left coming from the right, slide-right coming from the left, or scale-up growing from a smaller size)? A 5 means unambiguously the same direction; a 1 means a clearly different direction.

Is the magnitude of motion similar — the widget traverses roughly the same distance (or scales by roughly the same factor) as in the reference, within ~2× either way? A 5 means within ~25 %; a 1 means an order-of-magnitude smaller or larger displacement.

Does the agent's animation appear to fire on page load and play once, with the widget moving from start-state to settled-state across the five panels? A 5 means clearly load-triggered, single-shot. A 1 means no motion is captured by the strip (e.g. the widget is static across all five panels, or only changes on hover / scroll which the load-strip wouldn't capture).

Is the duration of the animation similar to the reference — the widget reaches its settled state at roughly the same panel (within ~50 % of the reference's pacing)? A 5 means matching pacing across the five panels; a 1 means dramatically faster (settled by panel 1) or slower (still moving at panel 5).

Does the widget end (panel 5) at the same final position, size, and opacity as the reference? A 5 means visually indistinguishable settled-state in the last panel; a 1 means the widget is at a clearly different final position, size, or opacity.
